Leptoderris crispifolia is a plant species belonging to the legume family, Fabaceae. It is a tropical plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, used for its medicinal properties. Leptoderris crispifolia has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
